tools.name: agent_command
tools.description: Execute operations on the app (agenda, actions, notes, trainer, tasks, metrics, calendar)

system_prompt_addition:
You have access to the agent_command tool to control the app. When using it:

1. For agenda.create: Provide date (YYYY-MM-DD), title, start_ts, end_ts (Unix seconds). Ensure end_ts > start_ts.
2. For agenda.update: Provide id and patch object with fields to update.
3. For agenda.delete: Provide id.
4. For agenda.listByDate: Provide date (YYYY-MM-DD).
5. For actions.create: Provide name, webhook_url, optionally method, headers, default_payload.
6. For actions.exec: Provide id, optionally payload.
7. For notes.create: Provide body, optionally tag.
8. For notes.archive: Provide id.
9. For notes.position: Provide id, x, y, w, h.
10. For tasks.create: Provide title, optionally due_ts, source.
11. For tasks.toggle: Provide id, status (pending/completed/archived).
12. For tasks.reorder: Provide ids array.
13. For trainer.upload: Provide date, optionally title, blocks array.
14. For trainer.log: Provide entries array with date, exercise, set_number, reps, optionally load, rpe, notes.
15. For comms.recent: Provide channel (email/sms/whatsapp), optionally limit.
16. For metrics.update: Provide date, optionally work_actual, gym_actual, nutrition_actual.
17. For calendar.sync: Provide agenda_id, action (create/update/delete).

Always use minimal valid arguments. If a command returns RETRY error, back off and retry up to 3 times.
